titles.en: Chapter on What Suffices for the Freed Person on the Eve of Arafah Regarding the Hajj of Islam

text: 2891 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ شِهَابٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ أَعْتَقَ عَشِيَّةَ عَرَفَةَ عَبْداً لَهُ قَالَ «يُجْزِي عَنِ اَلْعَبْدِ حَجَّةُ اَلْإِسْلاَمِ وَ يُكْتَبُ لِلسَّيِّدِ أَجْرَانِ ثَوَابُ اَلْعِتْقِ وَ ثَوَابُ اَلْحَجِّ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2891 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Shihab, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a man who freed his servant on the eve of Arafah.
Imam (as) said: "The Hajj of Islam suffices for the servant, and the master is rewarded twice: once for the act of freeing the servant and once for the reward of the Hajj."

text: 2892 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَمْلُوكٌ أُعْتِقَ يَوْمَ عَرَفَةَ قَالَ «إِذَا أَدْرَكَ أَحَدَ اَلْمَوْقِفَيْنِ فَقَدْ أَدْرَكَ اَلْحَجَّ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2892 - It was narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "A servant was freed on the Day of Arafah."
Imam (as) said: "If he reaches one of the two stations (Arafah or Muzdalifah), then he has caught the Hajj."
